titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/251

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=558 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:03:23.321016+00 | translation moa=559 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:03:24.807553+00

span.cl:
Nouerint uniuersi presens scriptum uisuri uel audituri quod ego iohannes de normanuill' dedi et concessi
et hac mea carta confirmaui deo et ecclesie sancte Marie de Melros . et Monachis ibidem deo seruien
tibus . In liberam . puram . et perpetuam elemosinam . quatuor acras terre et dimidiam . In territorio de
Mackustun’ . scilicet de ierbranderig ascendendo uersus occidentem desuper ripam de lillesietburne
ex aquilonali parte usque ad uetus fossatum de gretrig . et alibi in eodem territorio illam partem
more que iacet inclusa inter suthside . et arewes et ex orientali parte contigua est more de
Rutherforde . Totam hanc terram dedi prefatis monachis cum omnibus pertinenciis suis et libertati
bus sine aliquot retinemento vt eam habeant teneant et possideant plene honorifice et
quiete ab omni seruicio forensic et priuato . consuetudine . exactione . . . Ego et
heredes mei manutenebimus warantizabimus et defendemus dictis monachis dictam terram . . . in perpetuum contra omnes homines .

Hiis Testibus . Hugone de normanuill' . Thoma . Waleramo .
Henrico . fratribus meis . Iohanne capellano . Iohanne filio philippi et Multis aliis .

p.cl: Let everyone who shall view or hear the present writing know that I, John de Normanville, have given and granted and by this my charter made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, in free, pure, and perpetual alms, four-and-a-half acres of land in the territory of Maxton, that is to say, from 'Ierbranderig' going up westward above the bank of the 'Lillesiet' burn from the north side up to the old dike of 'Gretrig'; and elsewhere in the same territory, that part of the moor which lies confined between Southside and 'Arewes', and on the east side it is next to the moor of Rutherford. This entire land I have given to the afore-mentioned monks with all its pertinents and liberties without any with-holding so that they may have, hold and possess it fully, honourably and quit from all service forinsec and private, custom, exaction ... And I and my heirs shall maintain, warrant and defend to the said monks the said land ... for ever against all men.
With these witnesses: Hugh de Normanville, Thomas, Waleran, Henry, my brothers; John the chaplain; John son of Philip; and many others.
